name: Lemon Meringue Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Blend the pie crust mix, 1/4 cup sugar, and 1 egg in a medium-size bowl with a pastry blender.
3. Pat dough gently into a greased 13x9x2 inch baking pan.
4. Bake in a moderate (350 degree) oven for 10 minutes. Remove from the oven and place on a wire rack, leaving the oven on.
5. Beat the remaining 3 eggs and 1 cup sugar in a medium-size bowl with an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
6. Add lemon rind, juice, and flour; pour mixture over the pie crust.
7. Return to the oven and bake for 15 more minutes or until the meringue peaks are golden brown.
8. Remove from the oven and raise the temperature to very hot (450 degrees Fahrenheit).
9. Spread the meringue evenly over the lemon mixture.
10. Return to the oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes, or until the meringue is golden brown.
11. Remove from the oven and let cool on a wire rack.
12. Cut with a wet knife into bars.
(Note: The original recipe included additional notes about calories and serving size, but these were not part of the core recipe instructions.)
